definition: A saved way the program knows how to reach and open your database.
An organized collection of data managed by MySQL.
Describes data that is organized into tables and connected through relationships, allowing different sets of data to be linked and queried together.
A column, or group of columns, that uniquely identifies each row in a table.
A SQL command used to read, insert, update, or delete data.
A named database container in MySQL that stores tables, views, and other database objects.
A structure made of rows and columns that stores data inside a database.
A number a program uses to connect to a service on a computer. In MySQL, port 3306 is the usual number used to connect to the MySQL server.
The area in MySQL Workbench where users write and run SQL queries.
The table at the bottom of MySQL Workbench that displays the results of a query.
A single record in a table.
A vertical field in a table that stores one type of information.
A numeric datatype used to store whole numbers (integers) without decimal values.
A datatype used to store text of variable length, up to a specified number of characters, such as VARCHAR(45).
A datatype used to store calendar dates in the format YYYY-MM-DD.
A column constraint that requires a value to be entered and prevents the column from storing null (empty) values.
A column setting that automatically generates a unique number for each new row, increasing by 1 each time, and is typically used with a Primary Key.
A single box in a table where a row and column intersect, used to store one value for a specific column in a row.
The name or address of the computer or server that MySQL Workbench connects to. In this guide, `localhost` means your own computer.
A hostname that refers to your own computer. In this guide, it means MySQL Workbench is connecting to a MySQL server running on the same computer.
